title: Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu

seo.title: How to Make Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu — Easy Korean Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu. This easy Korean recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

easy Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu recipe
authentic Korean Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Korean Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu — a beloved Korean classic

description: Dubu Jorim is a beloved Korean side dish featuring pan-fried tofu simmered in a savory, spicy, and slightly sweet sauce. This comforting vegetarian recipe offers a delightful blend of textures and bold flavors, perfect for serving with a bowl of warm rice. It's a staple in many Korean households, known for its hearty and satisfying appeal.

instructions.title: Prepare the Tofu
instructions.text: Drain the tofu block and press it to remove excess water. You can do this by wrapping it in paper towels or a clean cloth and placing a heavy object on top for at least 15-20 minutes. Cut the pressed tofu into 1/2-inch thick rectangles or squares.

instructions.title: Pan-Fry the Tofu
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of cooking oil in a large non-stick pan over medium-high heat. Carefully place the tofu pieces in a single layer and pan-fry for 3-4 minutes per side, until golden brown and slightly crispy. Remove the fried tofu from the pan and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Braising Sauce
instructions.text: In a medium bowl, combine soy sauce, gochugaru, minced garlic, 1 chopped green onion, sesame oil, sugar, and 1 cup of water or vegetable broth. Stir well until all ingredients are thoroughly mixed and the sugar is dissolved.

instructions.title: Braise the Tofu
instructions.text: Arrange the pan-fried tofu pieces in a single layer in a wide, shallow pot or a deep skillet. Pour the prepared braising sauce evenly over the tofu. Ensure the tofu is mostly submerged in the sauce.

instructions.title: Simmer to Perfection
instructions.text: Bring the sauce to a gentle boil over medium-high heat, then reduce the heat to medium-low. Cover the pot and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the tofu has absorbed the flavors. Occasionally spoon the sauce over the tofu during simmering for even coating.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Once the sauce has reduced and thickened to your desired consistency, remove the pot from the heat. Garnish with the remaining chopped green onions and a sprinkle of sesame seeds. Serve immediately with warm rice and other Korean side dishes.

tips: Always press your tofu thoroughly to remove excess water. This step is crucial for achieving a firm texture and allowing the tofu to absorb the delicious braising sauce more effectively.
Adjust the amount of gochugaru (Korean chili flakes) to your preferred spice level. For a milder dish, use less; for extra heat, feel free to add a bit more.
Dubu Jorim tastes even better the next day as the flavors meld. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days.
article: Dubu Jorim Spicy Braised Tofu is a beloved Korean Vegetarian dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
